resistance_phenotype: V361A in PPX2 conferred resistance to fomesafen and lactofen in a transformed bacterial strain; the Alabama population without G210/R128 mutations showed fomesafen resistance ratios of 3.1 to 3.5.
evidence_type: genotyping; candidate mutation screening; transformed bacterial complementation/growth assay; greenhouse dose-response context
notes: Promote as a positive target-site resistance evidence row. Do not promote Delta G210, R128G/M, or G399A from this paper based only on this paper because those were primarily background/screening/context here.

resistance_phenotype: Mutation detected among Arkansas Palmer amaranth accessions surviving fomesafen screen; reported frequency: 82/167 accessions (49%).
evidence_type: whole-plant fomesafen screen; TaqMan qPCR genotyping; statewide field-accession survey
notes: Survey/association evidence for prevalent PPX2 target-site mechanisms in fomesafen-resistant Palmer amaranth; not a transformed functional assay.

resistance_phenotype: Mutation detected among Arkansas Palmer amaranth accessions surviving fomesafen screen; reported frequency: 47/167 accessions (28%).
evidence_type: whole-plant fomesafen screen; TaqMan qPCR genotyping; statewide field-accession survey
notes: Survey/association evidence for prevalent PPX2 target-site mechanisms in fomesafen-resistant Palmer amaranth; not a transformed functional assay.

resistance_phenotype: Mutation detected among Arkansas Palmer amaranth accessions surviving fomesafen screen; reported frequency: 3/167 accessions (rare).
evidence_type: whole-plant fomesafen screen; TaqMan qPCR genotyping; statewide field-accession survey
notes: Survey/association evidence for prevalent PPX2 target-site mechanisms in fomesafen-resistant Palmer amaranth; not a transformed functional assay.

resistance_phenotype: detected in 47% of resistant plants after fomesafen selection in Tennessee Palmer amaranth survey.
evidence_type: field fomesafen selection; TaqMan qPCR; dCAPS genotyping; survey of surviving plants
notes: All detected mutations were reported as heterozygous; no more than one of the three PPX2 mutations was detected in an individual surviving plant.

resistance_phenotype: accounted for 42% of resistance after fomesafen selection in Tennessee Palmer amaranth survey.
evidence_type: field fomesafen selection; TaqMan qPCR; dCAPS genotyping; survey of surviving plants
notes: All detected mutations were reported as heterozygous; no more than one of the three PPX2 mutations was detected in an individual surviving plant.

resistance_phenotype: accounted for 10% of resistance after fomesafen selection in Tennessee Palmer amaranth survey.
evidence_type: field fomesafen selection; TaqMan qPCR; dCAPS genotyping; survey of surviving plants

specific_herbicides_tested: fomesafen; saflufenacil; trifludimoxazin; field assays also included lactofen
resistance_phenotype: Biotype with Delta G210 showed resistance/susceptible ratios for fomesafen and saflufenacil but did not show reduced sensitivity to trifludimoxazin within species.
evidence_type: greenhouse dose-response; field experiments; resistant/susceptible ratio comparisons
notes: Positive evidence for fomesafen/saflufenacil resistance phenotype, and negative evidence for trifludimoxazin cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: Biotype with R128G showed resistance/susceptible ratios for fomesafen and saflufenacil but did not show reduced sensitivity to trifludimoxazin within species.
evidence_type: greenhouse dose-response; resistant/susceptible ratio comparisons
notes: Positive evidence for fomesafen/saflufenacil resistance phenotype, and negative evidence for trifludimoxazin cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: Biotype with Delta G210 showed resistance/susceptible ratios for fomesafen and saflufenacil but did not show reduced sensitivity to trifludimoxazin within species.

notes: Positive evidence for fomesafen/saflufenacil resistance phenotype, and negative evidence for trifludimoxazin cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: Biotype with V361A showed resistance/susceptible ratios for fomesafen and saflufenacil but did not show reduced sensitivity to trifludimoxazin within species.
evidence_type: greenhouse dose-response; resistant/susceptible ratio comparisons
notes: Positive evidence for fomesafen/saflufenacil resistance phenotype, and negative evidence for trifludimoxazin cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: Delta G210 was significantly associated with reduced injury/reduced relative efficacy of epyrifenacil in some Palmer amaranth accessions; follow-up showed epyrifenacil generally provided strong control of high-Delta G210 accessions.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; short-read PPX2 cDNA sequencing; same-plant association analysis
figure_or_table: Abstract; Table 1; Table 2; Figure 2; Table S2
notes: This is an epyrifenacil-efficacy association row, not proof of broad cross-resistance to all PPO inhibitors.

resistance_phenotype: Delta G210 was significantly associated with reduced injury/reduced relative efficacy of epyrifenacil in waterhemp, but epyrifenacil generally provided strong control in follow-up testing.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; short-read PPX2 cDNA sequencing; same-plant association analysis
figure_or_table: Abstract; Table 1; Table 2; Figure 2; Table S2
notes: This is an epyrifenacil-efficacy association row, not proof of broad cross-resistance to all PPO inhibitors.

resistance_phenotype: R128G was detected in Palmer amaranth, but the paper reported no significant effect on epyrifenacil efficacy in the association analysis.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; short-read PPX2 cDNA sequencing; same-plant association analysis
notes: Include as verified detection/negative epyrifenacil-effect evidence; do not interpret as epyrifenacil cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: R128G was detected in waterhemp, but the paper reported no significant effect on epyrifenacil efficacy in the association analysis.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; short-read PPX2 cDNA sequencing; same-plant association analysis
notes: Include as verified detection/negative epyrifenacil-effect evidence; do not interpret as epyrifenacil cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: V361A was detected in Palmer amaranth, but the paper reported no significant effect on epyrifenacil efficacy in the association analysis.

notes: Include as verified detection/negative epyrifenacil-effect evidence; do not interpret as epyrifenacil cross-resistance.

resistance_phenotype: G399A was detected in Palmer amaranth and was significantly associated with reduced injury in the epyrifenacil association analysis, but with smaller effect than Delta G210.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; short-read PPX2 cDNA sequencing; same-plant association analysis
notes: Epyrifenacil-specific association row; not a broad cross-resistance claim.

resistance_phenotype: R128G was novel to waterhemp in this survey and conferred fomesafen resistance in a bacterial system.
evidence_type: MiSeq amplicon sequencing; Sanger confirmation; bacterial functional complementation assay; sequence alignment
notes: Positive target-site resistance evidence for waterhemp R128G; paper also discusses gene flow with other Amaranthus species.

resistance_phenotype: R128I was novel to waterhemp in this survey and conferred fomesafen resistance in a bacterial system.
evidence_type: MiSeq amplicon sequencing; Sanger confirmation; bacterial functional complementation assay; sequence alignment
notes: Positive target-site resistance evidence for waterhemp R128I; paper also discusses gene flow with other Amaranthus species.

resistance_phenotype: R128K was detected in waterhemp but did not confer fomesafen resistance in the bacterial system in this paper.
evidence_type: MiSeq amplicon sequencing; Sanger confirmation; bacterial functional complementation assay
notes: Keep out of positive resistance-mutation lists unless new evidence is added; included here as a verified negative outcome.

resistance_phenotype: Accessions with high Delta G210 frequency and Delta G210 homozygous survivors had higher fomesafen resistance and cross-resistance potential to other PPO herbicides; saflufenacil and trifludimoxazin generally controlled populations.
evidence_type: greenhouse dose-response; cross-resistance assay; TaqMan genotyping of survivors
notes: Study focused on Delta G210 and G399A mutation patterns; do not promote R128 rows from this paper alone because R128 is background/context here.

resistance_phenotype: G399A occurred in selected PPO-resistant accessions and, especially with Delta G210 in some individuals/populations, was associated with cross-resistance potential to other PPO herbicides.
evidence_type: greenhouse dose-response; cross-resistance assay; TaqMan genotyping of survivors
notes: Evidence is population/accession-level and often involves coexistence with Delta G210; avoid interpreting this row as isolated single-mutation causality.

resistance_phenotype: R98G was found in PPX2 cDNA from resistant Palmer amaranth plants; no fomesafen-sensitive plants contained the mutation; paper concluded the new PPX2 mutations likely confer PPO-inhibitor resistance.
evidence_type: PPX2 cDNA sequencing; fomesafen selection; comparison with sensitive plants; dCAPS assay development
notes: Original paper reports R98G. Later literature may refer to homologous Amaranthus PPX2 residue numbering differently; do not merge with R128G until residue mapping is documented.

resistance_phenotype: R98M was found in PPX2 cDNA from resistant Palmer amaranth plants; no fomesafen-sensitive plants contained the mutation; paper concluded the new PPX2 mutations likely confer PPO-inhibitor resistance.
evidence_type: PPX2 cDNA sequencing; fomesafen selection; comparison with sensitive plants; dCAPS assay development
notes: Original paper reports R98M. Later literature may refer to homologous Amaranthus PPX2 residue numbering differently; do not merge with R128M until residue mapping is documented.

resistance_phenotype: Resistant genotype R2 harbored Arg-128-Gly in PPX2 and showed >20-fold fomesafen resistance; paper also found metabolic resistance components.
evidence_type: whole-plant bioassay; PPO enzyme activity assay; PPX2 sequencing; metabolism assay; transcriptome/RT-qPCR; heterologous expression for metabolic gene
figure_or_table: Abstract; Results; PPX2 sequence analysis section
notes: Other Amaranthus species, not the main Palmer/waterhemp focus; resistance is broad and includes metabolic mechanisms, so do not treat R128G as the only causal mechanism in this genotype.
